summary: Independent QA accepts M7-DECISION-INSPECTOR-REWRITE-001 for lead integration. The right inspector now leads with current problem, recommendation, cost, benefit, risk, next action, and localized reason chips before grouped decision data; population, labor, grain, cash, route, governance, obligation, appointment, and campaign data remain reachable under decision sections. R1 gameplay REQUEST_CHANGES was fixed in R2: unreachable-route guidance no longer points at a disabled Review obligations action, and default player inspector copy no longer exposes raw/read-model wording. The implementation remains presentation/client-test scoped, preserves existing command callbacks and preview/confirmation boundaries, and does not touch forbidden sim/protocol/save/content/Electron/manual-battle/server/telemetry/cloud-save/release/commercial/branding paths. This ACCEPT is QA review evidence only; lead_orchestrator must perform integration and task closure.

tests_run.result: Reviewed decision assistant, reason-code formatting, blocked-route i18n, action callbacks, and test assertions. rg was unavailable in this environment with Access denied, so Select-String was used as fallback.
Before writing this QA handoff, worktree remained clean on codex/m7-decision-inspector-rewrite.

decisions.decision: Accepted. The right inspector prioritizes current problem, recommendation, cost, benefit, risk, and next action before grouped district data; tests assert ordering and visible sections.
Accepted. Population, labor, grain, cash, route/campaign, governance/appointment, obligation, and campaign/postwar effects remain visible under decision data subsections.

Accepted for this task scope. Reviewed reason chips through i18n.formatReasonCode, added stable reason keys for reviewed route/campaign/grain/muster/governance/obligation/siege/withdrawal/postwar codes, and verified player-mode tests reject raw reason-code/internal-id leaks and R1 raw/read-model inspector wording.

Accepted. R1 gameplay REQUEST_CHANGES was addressed: blocked-route next action now tells the player to select a reachable district from the map or route queue first, and default inspector copy no longer says raw district read-model values.

Accepted. The inspector derives presentation from read models and only calls existing appointment/campaign/obligation callbacks. It does not import sim-core/protocol changes, duplicate domain rules, or mutate authoritative WorldState from UI.

Accepted. Diff scope is limited to allowed web/ui/test/process paths. No sim-core, protocol, save-format, content-source, Electron, manual node battle, server, telemetry, cloud-save, release, commercial, or branding changes were found.
Accepted. Writer R2, gameplay R2, and test handoffs validate; task JSON and thread fields coherently route from R1 REQUEST_CHANGES to R2 ACCEPT and test ACCEPT, with QA as the next reviewer.

risks.summary: Blocked-route guidance is text-level help that points to existing map/route queue selection surfaces, not a full route-repair tutorial.
risks.mitigation: Acceptable for this right-inspector recovery task; carry only if later UX smoke or player review still reports blocked-route confusion.

risks.summary: The i18n fallback still title-cases unknown reason codes. Current reviewed player paths and tests cover the known inspected codes, but future reason sources can regress if stable keys are not added.
risks.mitigation: Future UI tasks should keep adding explicit player-facing i18n keys and raw-key assertions for any newly surfaced reason source.

requested_action: Lead may integrate this QA ACCEPT and proceed with PR/task integration. Do not mark content lock, M7 exit, release readiness, manual node battle reversal, server/telemetry/cloud-save/commercial/branding scope, or final-art/cultural production approval from this review.
